newsSinceJun6: Three on-thesis positives: collaboration with NVIDIA to accelerate 800 VDC power architectures for next-gen AI data centers (MGX ecosystem expansion, ~Jun 8); launch of GaNEXUS gallium nitride power portfolio targeting AI data center power density (Jun 9); Elite Pairing Studio SiC MOSFET/gate-driver design tool (Jun 8). Today's -6.3% is flow-driven, not company-specific: chips retreated again on inflation jitters and pre-CPI positioning, the aftershock of the Jun 5 sector rout triggered by Broadcom's soft AI guidance and a hot 172k jobs print that killed near-term rate-cut hopes.

sources.label: onsemi PR - NVIDIA 800 VDC collaboration for AI data centers
Semiconductor Today - onsemi role in NVIDIA MGX 800VDC expanding (Jun 8)
StockTitan - GaNEXUS GaN portfolio launch, AI data center power hardware shrink up to 60%
QuiverQuant - onsemi slides as chips retreat on inflation jitters and pre-event positioning
CNN - Jun 5 chip-led rout on Broadcom guidance and rate-hike odds

verdicts.rankRationale: On a residual-alpha page, a -6.3% flow-driven flush paired with genuinely new power-bottleneck evidence (NVIDIA 800VDC collaboration, GaNEXUS launch) improves asymmetry versus rank-37 peers that only de-rated.
verdicts.bull: 800VDC is the architecture shift that multiplies power-semi content per AI rack, and onsemi just got named into the NVIDIA ecosystem for it; the pullback resets entry after the macro flush.
verdicts.neutral: At $43B and +100% YTD, onsemi is hardly hidden; the new collaborations are real but revenue timing into 2027 racks is unproven.
verdicts.bear: Auto/industrial still dominates the revenue mix, so a macro downturn plus rate-hike risk hits the core business while the AI power story is still optionality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Design-win or revenue quantification of the NVIDIA 800VDC/MGX power content at upcoming events or Q2 earnings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Renewed rate-hike repricing compresses semi multiples and the auto/industrial cycle stays soft into H2.

verdicts.rankRationale: The page's knock on ON was indirectness to AI nodes; the NVIDIA 800VDC/MGX ecosystem tie plus the GaNEXUS launch is a concrete directness upgrade that rank 89 does not reflect, and yesterday's down move pre-dated this evidence.
verdicts.bull: Named participation in NVIDIA's 800VDC power architecture moves onsemi from generic power supplier toward a specified node in next-gen AI rack power delivery.
verdicts.neutral: Power delivery remains a more substitutable layer than HBM, packaging, or test; multiple vendors (Infineon, TI, MPS, Navitas) are in the same 800VDC push.
verdicts.bear: If 800VDC adoption slips or NVIDIA spreads content across many suppliers, ON's incremental AI revenue stays too small to offset auto-cycle drag.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: MGX/800VDC reference designs shipping with onsemi SiC/GaN content specified.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded 800VDC supplier field dilutes content share and pricing.

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the CPO lens nothing changed: the NVIDIA news is power-side, not co-packaged optics, so rank 88 stays; the flow-driven dip only marginally improves the setup. Yesterday's down move already captured the rerating penalty.
verdicts.bull: Even on a CPO page, cheaper entry into a strengthening AI-power adjacency adds modest optionality if the list tolerates power/SiC satellites.
verdicts.neutral: ON remains a Power/SiC category holding with no direct CPO exposure; new announcements do not change its place in the optics bottleneck map.
verdicts.bear: A non-CPO name at +100% YTD with auto/industrial cyclicality is dead weight on an optics-bottleneck list if the macro flush continues.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any onsemi power content specified inside CPO-era rack architectures (800VDC sidecars for optics-dense switches).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Auto/industrial cycle deterioration with zero CPO offset.

verdicts.rankRationale: This page's thesis is power-delivery content, and the NVIDIA 800VDC collaboration plus GaNEXUS launch is exactly that evidence arriving while the stock gives back 6.3% on macro flow - asymmetry improved versus yesterday's rerating-driven down move.
verdicts.bull: 800VDC transition raises onsemi's dollar content per AI rack across SiC and GaN, and the NVIDIA tie validates the board-level power thesis with a named anchor customer ecosystem.
verdicts.neutral: Announcements are portfolio and ecosystem news, not booked revenue; the stock's +100% YTD already prices meaningful AI power success.
verdicts.bear: Power semis are competitive and cyclical; if hyperscaler capex pace disappoints (per Broadcom's guidance scare), the AI power TAM expands slower than the multiple assumes.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Quantified AI data center revenue or 800VDC design-win disclosure at Q2 2026 earnings.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Macro rate-hike repricing plus soft auto demand compresses both earnings and multiple simultaneously.
